children.description: Raw LLM text must be parsed into structured Python objects before your app can act on it. Without output parsers, you write fragile regex hacks that break the moment the model rephrases its output.

children.title: Conversation Memory
children.description: Without memory, every user message is treated as a fresh conversation. LangChain memory modules persist and inject conversation history, enabling coherent multi-turn interactions without manual history management.

children.title: Document Loaders
children.description: AI apps need to ingest PDFs, web pages, and databases as context. LangChain document loaders abstract file parsing and web scraping into a unified interface, so you spend time on logic, not format-specific parsers.

children.title: LangSmith Tracing
children.description: LangChain chains involve many nested calls that are invisible at runtime. LangSmith traces each step, capturing inputs, outputs, and latencies, making it the primary debugging tool when a chain returns unexpected results.

children.title: Callbacks & Token Logging
children.description: Callbacks let you hook into every chain step for logging, metrics, and cost tracking. Without them, you have zero observability into what prompts are being sent or how many tokens each step consumes.

children.title: LangChain Integrations
children.description: LangChain provides pre-built integrations for over 50 model providers, vector stores, and tool APIs. Using these integrations rather than raw clients saves hundreds of lines of boilerplate and ensures interface consistency.

children.title: LangGraph for Stateful Agents
children.description: LangChain's sequential chains are not enough for agents that loop, branch, or make decisions. LangGraph models agent logic as a graph with explicit state, enabling the complex control flows that production agents require.

ai-developer.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
ai-developer.description: Ground LLMs in your own data. Build a RAG pipeline that retrieves relevant context before answering.
ai-developer.challenge: Build a RAG chatbot over a folder of PDFs that answers with citations to the source.

children.title: Why RAG — Grounding & Freshness
children.description: LLMs are frozen at training time and hallucinate on domain-specific facts. RAG grounds answers in retrieved documents, making responses accurate, up-to-date, and attributable to source material.

children.title: Document Chunking Strategies
children.description: How you split documents directly determines retrieval quality. Chunks too large waste context window; chunks too small lose surrounding meaning. Poor chunking is the most common cause of bad RAG answers.

children.title: Similarity Search & Retrieval
children.description: The retrieval step fetches the most semantically relevant chunks for a query using cosine or dot-product similarity. Returning irrelevant chunks pollutes the context and causes the model to generate confidently wrong answers.

children.title: Context Injection & Prompting
children.description: Retrieved chunks must be injected into the prompt in a format the model can reason over. Injecting too many chunks exceeds the context window; injecting too few leaves critical information out of reach.

children.title: Citations & Source Tracking
children.description: Users need to verify AI answers against source documents. Without explicit source attribution in your RAG pipeline, answers are unauditable and trust erodes over time, especially in enterprise or regulated contexts.

children.title: Advanced Retrieval Techniques
children.description: Naive top-k retrieval misses relevant chunks when the query is underspecified. Techniques like query rewriting, multi-query retrieval, and re-ranking significantly improve recall for complex user questions.

children.title: Evaluating RAG Pipelines
children.description: RAG has two failure modes: retrieval failure and generation failure. Without separate evaluation of each step using metrics like context precision and answer faithfulness, you cannot tell which part of the pipeline to improve.

children.title: Hybrid Search
children.description: Pure semantic search misses exact keyword matches; pure keyword search misses semantic meaning. Combining both with hybrid search consistently outperforms either alone, especially for technical or named-entity queries.

ai-developer.title: Embeddings & Vector Stores
ai-developer.description: Power semantic search and retrieval. Generate embeddings and store them in a vector database.
ai-developer.challenge: Index a knowledge base in a vector DB with metadata filtering and build a semantic search endpoint.

children.title: Embedding Models & Dimensions
children.description: Embeddings are dense vector representations of meaning. Choosing an embedding model with the wrong dimensionality or training domain causes poor semantic similarity scores that make retrieval unreliable.

children.title: Similarity Metrics
children.description: Cosine similarity, dot product, and Euclidean distance each behave differently depending on whether vectors are normalized. Using the wrong metric for your vector store configuration silently degrades ranking quality.

children.title: Pinecone — Managed Vector DB
children.description: Pinecone provides a fully managed vector index with ANN search at scale. Without understanding namespaces, metadata filtering, and upsert batching, you will build inefficient indexes that grow expensive at production data volumes.

children.title: Chroma — Local Vector Store
children.description: Chroma is the fastest way to add vector search to a local or small-scale app without any cloud dependency. Understanding its collection API and persistence options prevents data loss between application restarts.

children.title: Metadata Filtering
children.description: Returning embeddings from the wrong document source or date range poisons RAG results. Metadata filtering lets you scope retrieval to the right subset before semantic ranking, combining precision and recall.

children.title: pgvector — Embeddings in Postgres
children.description: Running vector search inside your existing Postgres database eliminates a separate vector service. pgvector brings IVFFlat and HNSW indexes to SQL, enabling semantic search alongside your relational data in one query.

children.title: Batch Embedding & Indexing
children.description: Embedding large document corpora one-by-one is prohibitively slow and expensive. Batch embedding APIs amortize per-request overhead, cutting indexing time and cost by an order of magnitude for large knowledge bases.

children.title: HNSW & ANN Index Tuning
children.description: Approximate nearest-neighbor indexes trade recall for speed. Misconfiguring ef_construction or m parameters causes either slow build times or poor recall at query time, undermining the whole retrieval stack.

ai-developer.title: Building AI Agents & Tools
ai-developer.description: Let models take actions. Build agents that use tools, call functions and follow multi-step plans.
ai-developer.challenge: Build an agent that can search a database and call an external API to complete a task end to end.

children.title: The ReAct Pattern
children.description: ReAct interleaves reasoning and acting in a loop: the model reasons about what to do, calls a tool, observes the result, then reasons again. Without this loop, agents cannot handle multi-step tasks that require intermediate results.

children.title: Agent Loops & Planning
children.description: An agent loop runs the model repeatedly until a stop condition is met. Failing to set maximum iteration limits and detecting infinite loops causes runaway agents that burn through your API budget in seconds.

children.title: Defining Tools with JSON Schema
children.description: Tools are described to the model via JSON Schema. A poorly described parameter — missing description, wrong type, or no required fields — causes the model to call tools with invalid arguments or skip them entirely.

children.title: Multi-Tool Orchestration
children.description: Real agents need to coordinate search, computation, and API calls in sequence. Defining tools that return structured, composable outputs ensures the model can chain their results across reasoning steps without confusion.

children.title: Connecting External APIs
children.description: Agents derive their value from taking real-world actions — searching the web, querying databases, or calling third-party APIs. Each integration requires input validation and output normalization before the result re-enters the agent loop.

children.title: MCP — Model Context Protocol
children.description: MCP is an open standard for exposing tools and data sources to LLM agents. Building MCP-compatible tool servers lets any compliant agent host discover and call your tools without bespoke integration work.

children.title: Guarding Against Loops & Misuse
children.description: An unconstrained agent can loop indefinitely, call destructive APIs, or be manipulated through prompt injection in tool outputs. Iteration limits, tool allow-lists, and output sanitization are non-negotiable for production agents.

children.title: Human-in-the-Loop Checkpoints
children.description: Fully autonomous agents are high-risk in production. Inserting human approval steps before irreversible actions — sending emails, deleting records, making purchases — limits blast radius when the model makes a wrong decision.

ai-developer.title: Serving AI with FastAPI
ai-developer.description: Expose your AI features as a production API. Build endpoints with FastAPI, including streaming.
ai-developer.challenge: Wrap your RAG agent in a FastAPI service with a streaming chat endpoint and request validation.

children.title: Request Validation with Pydantic
children.description: FastAPI uses Pydantic models to validate incoming JSON automatically. Skipping validation means malformed payloads reach your LLM call, causing cryptic errors deep in the stack instead of clean 422 responses at the boundary.

children.title: Dependency Injection
children.description: FastAPI's dependency system injects shared resources — LLM clients, DB connections, auth tokens — into route handlers without global state. Bypassing it forces manual passing of shared objects and makes tests much harder to write.

children.title: Server-Sent Events in FastAPI
children.description: Streaming LLM output token-by-token requires SSE. Without it, the HTTP response is held open until generation completes, forcing users to wait seconds before seeing any output — a poor UX for chat applications.

children.title: WebSockets for Chat
children.description: HTTP request-response is one-directional; WebSockets enable full-duplex bidirectional communication. For interactive chat UIs that need server-initiated pushes or real-time updates, WebSockets are the correct transport.

children.title: Authentication & API Keys
children.description: Exposing LLM endpoints without auth passes your API costs to anyone who discovers the URL. FastAPI's security utilities make adding Bearer token or API key authentication straightforward with minimal boilerplate.

children.title: Rate Limiting AI Endpoints
children.description: LLM endpoints are expensive per call. Without rate limiting, a single abusive user can exhaust your monthly token budget in minutes. Slowapi or a reverse proxy rate limiter protects your service and controls costs.

children.title: Background Tasks & Queues
children.description: Long-running LLM generations should not block the HTTP thread. FastAPI background tasks and async task queues like Celery or ARQ decouple generation from the request cycle, improving throughput and user experience.

children.title: Testing FastAPI with LLM Mocks
children.description: Real LLM calls in tests are slow and costly. Mocking the model client in FastAPI's TestClient or pytest fixtures lets you test routing, validation, and error handling at full speed without touching external APIs.

ai-developer.title: Evaluation, Guardrails & Cost
ai-developer.description: Make AI apps reliable and affordable. Evaluate quality, add safety guardrails, and control cost.
ai-developer.challenge: Build an eval set for your RAG app, add input/output guardrails, and add a response cache.

children.title: Building an Eval Set
children.description: Without a golden test set, you cannot know whether a prompt change improved or degraded output quality. A curated set of input-expected-output pairs enables regression testing before every deployment.

children.title: LLM-as-Judge
children.description: Human evaluation does not scale. Using a stronger model to score outputs against criteria provides fast, automatable quality signals — though LLM judges can themselves be biased and require calibration.

children.title: Input & Output Guardrails
children.description: Without guardrails, users can extract harmful content or manipulate your AI into off-topic behavior. Input classifiers block malicious prompts before they reach the model; output classifiers catch harmful responses before they reach users.

children.title: Content Moderation
children.description: User-generated inputs to LLMs can contain toxic, illegal, or privacy-violating content. Integrating a moderation layer before the model call prevents policy violations and liability, especially in consumer-facing products.

children.title: Response Caching
children.description: Identical or semantically similar queries account for a large share of LLM traffic. Caching responses at the exact-match or semantic level can reduce token spend by 20-60% in production without any change to answer quality.

children.title: Token Optimization
children.description: Verbose system prompts, redundant context, and unused examples all inflate token counts and cost. Regularly profiling prompt sizes and trimming dead weight is a high-ROI optimization that compounds across millions of requests.

children.title: Logging Prompts & Responses
children.description: Without prompt-response logs, debugging production failures is guesswork. Structured logging of every LLM call with latency, token count, model version, and output enables root-cause analysis and quality trend tracking.

children.title: Detecting Output Drift
children.description: Model providers silently update models, causing outputs to shift over time. Monitoring key quality metrics against your eval set on a schedule surfaces regressions before users report them.

ai-developer.title: Deployment & Observability
ai-developer.description: Ship and operate AI applications. Containerize, trace requests, and deploy with confidence.
ai-developer.challenge: Containerize your AI app, add request tracing and cost logging, and deploy it via CI.

children.title: Dockerizing AI Services
children.description: AI apps have complex dependency trees including CUDA drivers, model weights, and native libraries. A well-structured Dockerfile with multi-stage builds produces reproducible images that run identically in CI and production.

children.title: Docker Compose for Local Dev
children.description: AI services depend on vector databases, caches, and queues running alongside them. Docker Compose orchestrates all services with a single command, eliminating the 'works on my machine' problem across the whole team.

children.title: Distributed Tracing with OpenTelemetry
children.description: A user request in an AI app may fan out across the LLM, vector DB, and multiple microservices. OpenTelemetry traces the full call graph with timings, making it possible to find which step is responsible for latency spikes.

children.title: LangSmith for LLM Tracing
children.description: Generic APM tools do not understand prompt-response semantics. LangSmith captures the full LLM-specific trace — prompt, completion, token counts, cost, model version — giving you the observability layer AI apps actually need.

children.title: Deploying to Cloud Platforms
children.description: Container registries, environment variable injection, and auto-scaling differ across AWS, GCP, and Azure. Knowing the deployment primitives for at least one cloud — ECS, Cloud Run, or Azure Container Apps — is essential for shipping AI services.

children.title: Secret Management in Cloud
children.description: Hard-coding API keys in container images is a critical security failure. Cloud secret managers inject secrets at runtime as environment variables, keeping credentials out of container images and version control.

children.title: CI/CD Pipelines with GitHub Actions
children.description: Manual deployments of AI services introduce inconsistency and delay. A CI pipeline that runs your eval set on every PR, builds the Docker image, and pushes to the registry on merge ensures every deployment is tested and reproducible.

children.title: Cost & Latency Monitoring
children.description: LLM costs and latencies fluctuate with traffic, model updates, and prompt changes. Setting up cost dashboards and latency alerts lets you catch budget overruns and degraded performance before they become business problems.

nlp-engineer.title: NLP Foundations
nlp-engineer.description: Start with how machines process language. Understand the core tasks and challenges of natural language processing.
nlp-engineer.challenge: Build a simple text-stats tool that tokenizes a corpus and reports vocabulary and frequencies.

children.title: Python for NLP
children.description: Python is the de-facto language of NLP research and production. Without fluent Python — comprehensions, generators, string handling — every downstream NLP library becomes a black box you cannot debug or extend.

children.title: Computational Linguistics Primer
children.description: Understanding morphology, syntax, and semantics tells you what structure NLP models are actually trying to learn. Engineers who skip linguistics repeatedly reinvent solutions already solved by decades of linguistic theory.

resources.label: NLTK Book — Language Processing and Python (Ch. 1)

children.title: Text Representation Fundamentals
children.description: Computers work with numbers, not words. Knowing how raw text gets mapped to tokens and integers is the foundation every model, embedding, and tokenizer builds on — misunderstand it and debugging becomes guesswork.

children.title: Pipeline Thinking in NLP
children.description: Real NLP systems are pipelines: raw text flows through preprocessing, feature extraction, modeling, and post-processing. Understanding data flow end-to-end prevents silent errors where one broken stage corrupts everything downstream.

resources.label: spaCy — 101: Everything You Need to Know

children.title: Virtual Environments & Package Management
children.description: NLP projects combine dozens of libraries with conflicting dependencies. Isolated virtual environments prevent version collisions and ensure reproducible installs across machines and teammates.

children.title: Jupyter for NLP Experimentation
children.description: Notebooks let you iterate on text transformations interactively, inspect intermediate outputs, and mix prose with code. Almost every NLP tutorial and paper prototype lives in a notebook first.

resources.label: Kaggle — Getting Started with NLP Notebooks

children.title: Probability & Statistics
children.description: Language models are probability distributions over sequences. Concepts like conditional probability, entropy, and Bayes' theorem underpin everything from n-gram models to modern transformers — skip them and model outputs become magic you cannot reason about.

children.title: Linear Algebra for Embeddings
children.description: Embeddings are vectors in high-dimensional space; similarity is a dot product; attention is matrix multiplication. Without linear algebra intuition you cannot interpret why models succeed or fail on specific inputs.

nlp-engineer.title: Text Preprocessing
nlp-engineer.description: Clean text is the foundation of NLP. Master tokenization, normalization and the preprocessing pipeline.
nlp-engineer.challenge: Build a reusable preprocessing pipeline and compare word-level vs subword tokenization.

children.title: Word & Subword Tokenization
children.description: How you split text determines the vocabulary size and the model's ability to handle unknown words. Subword methods like BPE and WordPiece are used in every modern transformer — misapplying tokenization causes silent data leakage and inflated benchmarks.

children.title: Sentence & Document Segmentation
children.description: Models have fixed context windows, so splitting a document into coherent chunks is critical. Poor sentence boundaries corrupt training examples and produce incoherent model outputs, especially for long-document tasks.

children.title: Normalization & Case Folding
children.description: Inconsistent casing, accents, and Unicode variants cause the same word to appear as thousands of distinct tokens. Normalization reduces vocabulary size and makes models generalize better across writing styles and languages.

children.title: Stopword Removal & Stemming
children.description: Removing high-frequency function words and reducing words to roots compresses the feature space for classical models. Applying these blindly to transformer inputs, however, destroys contextual signals — knowing when not to normalize is as important as knowing how.

children.title: Regex for Pattern Extraction
children.description: Regex is the first line of structured extraction before any ML model — dates, emails, phone numbers, and domain-specific patterns are cheapest to capture with regex. Engineers who skip regex add unnecessary model complexity to trivially solvable problems.

children.title: Regex in spaCy Rule-Based Matching
children.description: spaCy's Matcher and EntityRuler let you combine regex with linguistic annotations for high-precision extraction. Hybrid rule-model systems consistently outperform pure neural approaches in low-data, high-precision production settings.

children.title: Corpus Inspection & Deduplication
children.description: Duplicate training examples bias models toward memorized outputs rather than generalizable patterns. Inspecting your corpus before training reveals label noise and distribution skew that accounts for most unexplained performance gaps.

children.title: Train/Val/Test Splitting for Text
children.description: Random splits of text data often leak information across splits when documents share author style or topic. Stratified and time-based splits prevent optimistic benchmark scores that collapse in production.

resources.label: Practical NLP — Data Splitting Best Practices

nlp-engineer.title: Classical NLP
nlp-engineer.description: Before transformers, classical methods solved real problems — and still do. Learn the techniques and when they shine.
nlp-engineer.challenge: Build a TF-IDF + classifier pipeline for sentiment and compare it to a spaCy NER extractor.

children.title: spaCy Pipelines & Linguistic Annotations
children.description: spaCy delivers POS tags, dependency parses, and named entities in one efficient pipeline call. Misunderstanding pipeline order — and which components are active — is the most common source of silent annotation errors in production NLP services.

children.title: Custom spaCy Components
children.description: Real-world text rarely fits pre-trained models perfectly. Custom components let you inject domain rules, custom tokenizers, and task-specific annotators into the standard pipeline without forking library code.

children.title: NLTK Corpora & Lexical Resources
children.description: NLTK bundles WordNet, treebanks, and dozens of annotated corpora for research and prototyping. Knowing which corpora exist prevents duplicate data collection effort and gives you baselines to compare against.

resources.label: NLTK Book — Ch. 2: Accessing Text Corpora

children.title: Part-of-Speech Tagging & Parsing
children.description: POS tags and parse trees are the grammatical skeleton of a sentence. Feature engineering for classical models and rule extraction for information retrieval both depend on accurate syntactic annotation.

children.title: TF-IDF Vectorization
children.description: TF-IDF is still the fastest, most interpretable baseline for text classification and retrieval. Skipping it in favor of transformers on small datasets routinely produces worse results at 100x the compute cost.

children.title: Classical Text Classification
children.description: Naive Bayes and logistic regression on TF-IDF features remain strong baselines for sentiment analysis, spam detection, and topic categorization. Beating these baselines is the minimum bar that justifies neural model complexity.

resources.label: scikit-learn — Working with Text Data Tutorial

children.title: Precision, Recall & F1
children.description: Accuracy alone is misleading on imbalanced text datasets. Precision and recall trade-offs determine whether your system is better tuned for safety-critical (high recall) or precision-sensitive (high precision) deployment contexts.

children.title: Confusion Matrix Analysis
children.description: A confusion matrix reveals which classes your model systematically confuses, guiding targeted data collection or rule-based post-processing fixes that beat expensive retraining cycles.

resources.label: Towards Data Science — How to Plot Confusion Matrix

nlp-engineer.title: Word Embeddings
nlp-engineer.description: Represent meaning as vectors. Learn how embeddings capture semantic relationships between words.
nlp-engineer.challenge: Train word embeddings on a corpus and explore the nearest neighbors and analogies they learn.

children.title: Word2Vec Skip-gram & CBOW
children.description: Word2Vec popularized the idea that semantic relationships emerge from co-occurrence statistics. Understanding its objective function is the conceptual foundation for every modern embedding technique including transformer attention.

children.title: GloVe — Global Vectors
children.description: GloVe captures global corpus statistics rather than local windows, producing more stable embeddings for rare words. Knowing when GloVe outperforms Word2Vec (and vice versa) prevents wasted fine-tuning effort.

resources.label: Towards Data Science — GloVe vs Word2Vec

children.title: Cosine Similarity & Nearest Neighbors
children.description: Semantic search and recommendation systems rely on fast nearest-neighbor lookup in embedding spaces. A poor similarity metric or unscaled embeddings cause semantically related queries to return unrelated results.

children.title: Embedding Bias & Evaluation
children.description: Pre-trained embeddings encode societal biases that surface as discriminatory model outputs. Probing and debiasing embeddings before deployment is now a regulatory and reputational requirement in many industries.

children.title: Loading & Fine-tuning Pre-trained Embeddings
children.description: Training embeddings from scratch requires billions of tokens. Loading pre-trained vectors and fine-tuning on domain data converges faster and generalizes better than random initialization for most NLP tasks.

children.title: Sentence & Document Embeddings
children.description: Word embeddings cannot represent sentence-level meaning without aggregation. Sentence-BERT and similar models produce fixed-size sentence representations that capture semantic similarity far better than averaged word vectors.

children.title: t-SNE & UMAP for Embedding Visualization
children.description: High-dimensional embeddings are impossible to inspect directly. Dimensionality reduction to 2D reveals cluster structure, outliers, and domain shift — all critical for diagnosing model failures before they reach users.

children.title: Embedding Projector & Probing
children.description: Probing classifiers reveal what linguistic properties (syntax, tense, entity type) each layer of a model encodes. This knowledge guides which layers to freeze and which to fine-tune, directly affecting downstream task performance.

resources.label: Towards Data Science — Probing NLP Models

nlp-engineer.title: Deep Learning for NLP
nlp-engineer.description: Add neural networks to your toolkit. Build sequence models with PyTorch for language tasks.
nlp-engineer.challenge: Build and train an LSTM text classifier in PyTorch and evaluate it against a classical baseline.

children.title: RNNs & Vanishing Gradients
children.description: RNNs process sequences token-by-token and propagate state across time steps. Understanding vanishing gradients — why plain RNNs fail on long sequences — is the prerequisite for understanding why LSTMs and transformers were invented.

children.title: LSTMs & GRUs
children.description: LSTMs introduce gating mechanisms that selectively remember and forget context over long sequences. They remain the baseline for time-series NLP tasks like speech recognition where sequential order is strictly enforced by the data.

resources.label: Towards Data Science — LSTM for NLP

children.title: Tensors & Autograd
children.description: PyTorch's autograd engine automatically differentiates tensor operations, making custom NLP architectures trainable without hand-deriving gradients. Misunderstanding in-place operations and computational graph detachment causes silent gradient corruption.

children.title: DataLoader & Custom Datasets
children.description: Efficient batching and on-the-fly tokenization through DataLoader is what separates research prototypes from production-grade training pipelines. Incorrect padding masks and attention masks here cause models to attend to garbage tokens.

children.title: Loss Functions for Sequence Tasks
children.description: Cross-entropy loss for classification and CTC loss for sequence-to-sequence tasks behave very differently when labels are noisy or imbalanced. Choosing the wrong loss is the single most common reason NLP models plateau at mediocre performance.

resources.label: Towards Data Science — Loss Functions in NLP

children.title: Gradient Clipping & Learning Rate Scheduling
children.description: Recurrent networks are especially prone to exploding gradients on long sequences. Gradient clipping and warmup schedules are non-optional for stable LSTM and transformer training — omitting them causes training divergence that looks like a data problem.

children.title: Encoder-Decoder Architecture
children.description: Seq2seq with encoder-decoder is the backbone of machine translation, summarization, and dialogue systems. Understanding information bottlenecks in the context vector directly motivates why attention mechanisms were developed.

resources.label: Jay Alammar — Visualizing Neural Machine Translation

children.title: Beam Search & Decoding Strategies
children.description: Greedy decoding produces fast but suboptimal sequences; beam search explores multiple hypotheses simultaneously. Understanding decoding strategies is essential for tuning generation quality, diversity, and latency trade-offs in production.

resources.label: Towards Data Science — Beam Search Explained

nlp-engineer.title: Transformers & Attention
nlp-engineer.description: Understand the architecture that changed NLP. Learn self-attention and the transformer that powers modern models.
nlp-engineer.challenge: Implement scaled dot-product attention from scratch and explain each step.

children.title: Scaled Dot-Product Attention
children.description: Attention lets each token dynamically weight its relationship to every other token in the sequence. Without understanding the query-key-value formulation and the scaling factor, debugging attention-based models and interpreting their outputs is impossible.

resources.label: Attention Is All You Need (original paper)

children.title: Multi-Head Attention
children.description: Multiple attention heads allow a model to simultaneously attend to different representation subspaces — syntax in one head, coreference in another. Pruning or misunderstanding head roles leads to catastrophic forgetting during fine-tuning.

children.title: Positional Encoding
children.description: Unlike RNNs, transformers have no inherent notion of order. Positional encodings inject sequence position information; choosing sinusoidal vs learned encodings affects generalization to sequence lengths unseen during training.

children.title: Feed-Forward Sublayers & Layer Normalization
children.description: The FFN sublayer doubles each token's representation capacity after attention; layer norm stabilizes training by preventing internal covariate shift. Skipping pre-norm vs post-norm distinctions causes instability in deep transformer stacks.

children.title: BERT Pre-training Objectives
children.description: BERT's masked language modeling and next-sentence prediction create deeply bidirectional representations. Understanding these objectives explains why BERT excels at understanding tasks (classification, NER) but requires a separate decoder for generation.

children.title: BERT Variants: RoBERTa, DistilBERT, ALBERT
children.description: RoBERTa removes NSP and trains longer; DistilBERT distills to 40% fewer parameters; ALBERT shares weights across layers. Choosing the wrong variant wastes compute or sacrifices accuracy for no gain in your specific deployment context.

children.title: Causal Language Modeling
children.description: GPT-style models predict the next token given only left context, making them natural generators. Confusing causal (GPT) with masked (BERT) architectures when selecting a base model for your task is one of the most expensive mistakes in applied NLP.

children.title: T5 & Encoder-Decoder Transformers
children.description: T5 frames every NLP task as text-to-text, unifying classification, translation, and summarization under one interface. This paradigm dominates modern NLP APIs and knowing its input-output format prevents task formulation errors.

nlp-engineer.title: Hugging Face Transformers
nlp-engineer.description: Use the de facto NLP library. Load pretrained models and run real tasks with the Transformers ecosystem.
nlp-engineer.challenge: Use pretrained models to build a multi-task demo: classification, NER and summarization.

children.title: AutoModel & AutoTokenizer
children.description: HuggingFace's Auto classes load the correct architecture and tokenizer from a model hub identifier without hardcoding class names. Bypassing Auto classes leads to tokenizer-model mismatches that silently corrupt every prediction.

children.title: HuggingFace Model Hub
children.description: The Model Hub hosts 500,000+ pre-trained models with model cards, licenses, and benchmark scores. Ignoring model cards leads to deploying models with usage restrictions or unexpected domain limitations into production.

children.title: HuggingFace Pipelines for Inference
children.description: The pipeline() API wraps tokenization, forward pass, and post-processing into a single call. It is the fastest path from model hub to working prototype, but its default settings (batch size, device) cause OOM errors and slow throughput in production.

children.title: Batching & Device Management
children.description: Running pipelines one sample at a time on CPU is 50-200x slower than batched GPU inference. Correct device placement and batch size tuning are the most impactful optimizations before any model quantization or distillation.

children.title: Loading & Streaming Datasets
children.description: HuggingFace Datasets loads benchmark datasets in one line and streams datasets too large to fit in memory. Downloading entire datasets when streaming is available wastes hours of setup time and gigabytes of disk space.

children.title: Dataset Preprocessing & Mapping
children.description: The map() function applies tokenization and feature extraction in parallel with caching, avoiding repeated preprocessing across training runs. Preprocessing inside the training loop multiplies wall-clock time by the number of epochs.

children.title: Fast Tokenizers & Offset Mapping
children.description: HuggingFace's Rust-backed fast tokenizers return character-level offset mappings that align tokens back to the original text. Without offsets, NER and span extraction models cannot convert token-level predictions back to character positions for downstream consumers.

children.title: Special Tokens & Padding Strategies
children.description: CLS, SEP, PAD, and MASK tokens have model-specific roles that vary between BERT, RoBERTa, and GPT families. Incorrect special token handling silently degrades classification accuracy and breaks attention mask alignment.

nlp-engineer.title: Fine-Tuning & Transfer Learning
nlp-engineer.description: Adapt models to your domain. Fine-tune transformers efficiently with transfer learning and PEFT.
nlp-engineer.challenge: Fine-tune a transformer on a domain dataset with LoRA and beat the zero-shot baseline.

children.title: Task-Specific Heads & Classification
children.description: Fine-tuning adds a task-specific head (linear layer, CRF, etc.) on top of a pre-trained encoder. Freezing the wrong layers or learning rate imbalance between head and backbone is the most common cause of fine-tuning underperformance.

children.title: Learning Rate Warmup & Schedulers
children.description: Transformer weights are sensitive to the initial learning rate spike; linear warmup prevents early catastrophic forgetting. Using a flat learning rate without warmup on a pre-trained model consistently degrades the final accuracy by 2-5% on benchmark tasks.

resources.label: Towards Data Science — Warmup for Transformers

children.title: LoRA — Low-Rank Adaptation
children.description: LoRA injects trainable low-rank matrices into attention layers, reducing trainable parameters by 10,000x while matching full fine-tune quality. Without PEFT methods, fine-tuning large models requires GPU memory most teams cannot afford.

children.title: Prompt Tuning & Prefix Tuning
children.description: Prompt and prefix tuning prepend learned soft tokens to the input without modifying model weights, enabling multi-task adaptation with a single frozen backbone. Misconfigurating the number of virtual tokens wastes parameters with no performance gain.

children.title: Trainer & TrainingArguments
children.description: The Trainer handles mixed precision, gradient accumulation, evaluation loops, and checkpointing out of the box. Reimplementing these from scratch introduces subtle bugs (gradient accumulation step misalignment, eval metric averaging) that are hard to catch without reference code.

children.title: Custom Compute Metrics & Callbacks
children.description: The Trainer's compute_metrics hook and Callback system let you inject task-specific metrics (F1, BLEU, ROUGE) and early stopping without subclassing. Monitoring only loss during fine-tuning misses task-specific regressions that only appear in task metrics.

children.title: GLUE & SuperGLUE Benchmarks
children.description: GLUE and SuperGLUE are the standard benchmarks for comparing NLP model quality across diverse tasks. Reporting results on a single held-out test set without comparing to benchmark baselines makes it impossible to know if your fine-tuned model is competitive.

children.title: Error Analysis & Failure Modes
children.description: Aggregate metrics hide systematic failures on specific demographic groups, input lengths, or rare labels. Slice-based error analysis before deployment is the difference between a model that works in demos and one that works for all users.

nlp-engineer.title: NLP Tasks in Production
nlp-engineer.description: Apply NLP to real product problems. Implement the high-value tasks teams actually ship.
nlp-engineer.challenge: Build a document QA system that retrieves passages and extracts precise answers.

children.title: NER with Transformers
children.description: Token-level NER requires careful handling of subword tokenization: one word can split into multiple tokens, requiring label alignment. Ignoring subword-label alignment corrupts training targets and produces systematically wrong entity boundaries.

children.title: IOB2 & BIOES Labeling Schemes
children.description: Entity spans are encoded with BIO/BIOES tags that distinguish entity starts from continuations. Using inconsistent tagging schemes between training and inference causes entity merging errors that are invisible in token-level accuracy metrics.

resources.label: Towards Data Science — NER Tagging Schemes

children.title: Extractive QA with SQuAD
children.description: Extractive QA models predict start and end token spans within a context passage. Off-by-one errors in span indexing and tokenizer offset handling are the primary source of incorrect answer extraction despite high model confidence.

children.title: Retrieval-Augmented QA
children.description: Open-domain QA requires retrieving relevant documents before reading them. The retriever quality is typically the bottleneck: a poor retriever means the reader never sees the correct evidence, making reader improvements irrelevant.

children.title: Abstractive Summarization
children.description: Abstractive models generate novel sentences rather than extracting existing ones, requiring encoder-decoder architectures like BART or T5. Evaluating only with ROUGE scores misses factual inconsistency — the most critical failure mode for production summarizers.

children.title: ROUGE Evaluation
children.description: ROUGE measures n-gram overlap between generated and reference summaries and is the standard summarization metric in papers. However, high ROUGE does not guarantee factual correctness, making human evaluation essential before any user-facing deployment.

children.title: Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis
children.description: Product reviews often contain conflicting sentiments about different aspects. Document-level sentiment models collapse these signals into a single label, destroying actionable insight that aspect-based analysis preserves for product teams.

children.title: Zero-Shot & Few-Shot Classification
children.description: Zero-shot classifiers built on NLI models assign labels never seen during training. They eliminate the need for labeled data collection in new domains, but require carefully designed hypothesis templates whose wording dramatically affects accuracy.

nlp-engineer.title: Deployment & MLOps for NLP
nlp-engineer.description: Ship NLP models efficiently. Optimize, serve and monitor them in production.
nlp-engineer.challenge: Optimize your fine-tuned model with ONNX and serve it behind a FastAPI endpoint in Docker.

children.title: FastAPI for NLP Microservices
children.description: FastAPI's async request handling and automatic OpenAPI docs make it the standard framework for wrapping NLP models as REST APIs. Blocking inference inside async routes serializes all requests — a single slow prediction blocks every concurrent user.

children.title: HuggingFace Inference API & Endpoints
children.description: HuggingFace Endpoints deploy any Hub model as a managed, autoscaling endpoint without infrastructure setup. Skipping it in favor of self-hosted solutions incurs weeks of DevOps work that the Endpoints service eliminates for prototypes and medium-scale production.

children.title: Exporting to ONNX
children.description: ONNX exports a PyTorch model to a hardware-agnostic computation graph that runtimes like ONNX Runtime execute 2-5x faster than native PyTorch on CPU. Missing dynamic axes in the export config causes shape errors when request batch sizes vary.

children.title: Quantization for NLP
children.description: INT8 quantization reduces model size by 4x and inference latency by 2-3x with less than 1% accuracy loss on most NLP tasks. Applying quantization without calibration on representative data causes catastrophic accuracy drops on tail distributions.

children.title: Docker for NLP Services
children.description: Docker containers package the model, runtime, and all dependencies into a reproducible image that runs identically on any machine. Without containerization, environment drift between development and production causes inference discrepancies that are nearly impossible to reproduce.

children.title: Model Versioning & Experiment Tracking
children.description: Every fine-tuned checkpoint should be linked to the dataset, hyperparameters, and evaluation metrics that produced it. Without experiment tracking, teams repeatedly retrain models that already exist, wasting GPU budget and failing to reproduce best results.

children.title: Data Drift & Model Degradation
children.description: Language evolves and user behavior shifts — a model trained on last year's text can degrade silently on current inputs. Monitoring prediction confidence distributions and label drift is the earliest signal of model degradation before user complaints arrive.

children.title: Latency Profiling & Throughput Optimization
children.description: End-to-end latency for NLP APIs includes tokenization, inference, and post-processing. Profiling each stage reveals where optimization effort pays off — tokenization bottlenecks are more common than expected and are missed when only measuring model inference time.

cv-engineer.title: Image Processing Foundations
cv-engineer.description: Computer vision starts with understanding images as data. Learn how pixels, channels and color spaces work.
cv-engineer.challenge: Load an image, convert color spaces, and apply thresholding to segment a simple object.

children.title: Images as NumPy Arrays
children.description: Every image is a multidimensional array of integer values. Understanding the shape (H, W, C) and dtype directly affects how you index, slice, and transform images without silent errors or clipping bugs.

children.title: Color Spaces — RGB, HSV, Grayscale
children.description: RGB is display-friendly, HSV is segmentation-friendly, and grayscale halves your channel count. Choosing the wrong space makes thresholding brittle and color filtering unpredictable.

children.title: Image Histograms
children.description: Histograms reveal the intensity distribution of an image and are the foundation for contrast enhancement and exposure correction. Misreading them leads to models trained on poorly normalised inputs.

children.title: Thresholding & Binarisation
children.description: Global and adaptive thresholding converts grayscale images into binary masks essential for contour detection and object isolation. Getting the threshold wrong destroys downstream segmentation.

children.title: Geometric Transformations
children.description: Scaling, rotating, and flipping are the most common pre-processing steps for both display and model input. Applying transformations incorrectly changes spatial semantics and breaks label alignment.

children.title: Resolution & Sampling
children.description: Downsampling without anti-aliasing creates aliasing artefacts; upsampling with the wrong interpolation blurs edges. Both affect model accuracy and inference speed in production pipelines.

children.title: Reading & Writing Images
children.description: OpenCV reads images in BGR by default, not RGB — passing them to other libraries without conversion causes systematically wrong colours throughout your model pipeline.

children.title: Working with Video Streams
children.description: VideoCapture opens files, webcams, and RTSP streams with the same interface. Mishandling frame buffering or not releasing captures causes memory leaks in long-running inference services.

cv-engineer.title: OpenCV Essentials
cv-engineer.description: OpenCV is the workhorse of classical CV. Master the operations you'll use in every pipeline.
cv-engineer.challenge: Build a document scanner that detects edges, finds the page contour and warps it flat.

children.title: Spatial Filtering & Blurring
children.description: Gaussian, median, and bilateral filters smooth images or remove noise before edge detection. Using the wrong filter smears edges or leaves noise that creates false contours downstream.

children.title: Canny Edge Detection
children.description: Canny applies gradient magnitude and non-maximum suppression to find clean, thin edges. Incorrect hysteresis thresholds either miss edges or produce noisy, disconnected results.

children.title: Erosion, Dilation & Morphology
children.description: Morphological ops clean binary masks by removing small blobs or filling holes. Over-eroding destroys fine structures; under-dilating leaves gaps that break contour analysis.

children.title: Contours & Shape Analysis
children.description: Contours are the boundary curves of connected components and drive object counting, area measurement, and shape classification. Hierarchy misuse causes nested contour confusion.

children.title: Affine & Perspective Transforms
children.description: Affine transforms handle translation, rotation, and shear; perspective transforms correct projective distortion. Incorrect homography estimation produces warped outputs that ruin document scanning and AR overlays.

children.title: Hough Transform for Lines & Circles
children.description: The Hough transform detects parametric shapes like lines and circles in edge images. Incorrect accumulator thresholds produce missed detections or floods of false positives.

children.title: Background Subtraction
children.description: MOG2 and KNN background subtractors separate moving foreground from static scenes in video. Poor initialisation periods cause the background model to incorrectly absorb moving objects.

children.title: Drawing & Annotation
children.description: Drawing bounding boxes, text, and shapes onto frames is essential for debugging and visualisation. Forgetting to copy the frame before drawing mutates original data, corrupting downstream processing.

cv-engineer.title: Feature Detection & Classical CV
cv-engineer.description: Learn how classical CV finds and matches structure in images — the basis of tracking and stitching.
cv-engineer.challenge: Stitch two overlapping photos into a panorama using feature matching and homography.

children.title: Harris Corner Detector
children.description: Harris detects image corners by analysing the local intensity gradient structure. It is the foundation for understanding why keypoints are repeatable and invariant, which every modern detector builds upon.

children.title: SIFT & ORB Descriptors
children.description: SIFT is scale and rotation invariant; ORB is faster and patent-free. Choosing the wrong descriptor for your speed vs quality trade-off leads to either missed matches or real-time bottlenecks.

children.title: Brute-Force & FLANN Matching
children.description: Brute-force matching is exact but slow; FLANN uses approximate nearest neighbours for speed. Without ratio testing (Lowe's test) you get a flood of false matches that corrupt alignment.

children.title: Homography & Image Stitching
children.description: RANSAC-based homography estimation tolerates outlier matches to compute the geometric transform between images. A bad homography produces ghost artefacts and misaligned panoramas.

children.title: Lucas-Kanade Optical Flow
children.description: Lucas-Kanade tracks sparse keypoints between frames to estimate motion vectors. Without good initialisation or when the aperture problem applies, tracking drifts and loses the target.

children.title: Dense Optical Flow (Farneback)
children.description: Dense optical flow computes a motion vector for every pixel, useful for action recognition and video stabilisation. High computational cost makes frame-rate budgeting essential in real-time systems.

children.title: Intrinsic & Extrinsic Parameters
children.description: Camera calibration recovers the focal length, principal point, and distortion coefficients needed to un-distort images and reconstruct 3D geometry. Skipping calibration causes systematic measurement errors.

children.title: Stereo Vision & Depth
children.description: Stereo calibration rectifies image pairs so disparity search is a simple horizontal scan. Incorrect rectification makes depth estimates wrong by an order of magnitude in robotics and AR applications.

cv-engineer.title: Deep Learning with PyTorch
cv-engineer.description: Modern CV is deep learning. Build the PyTorch foundation you'll use to train vision models.
cv-engineer.challenge: Train a small neural network on an image dataset in PyTorch with a proper training loop.

children.title: PyTorch Tensors
children.description: Tensors are the fundamental data structure in PyTorch; understanding their shape, dtype, and device placement prevents the silent broadcasting bugs and GPU/CPU mismatch errors that dominate debugging time.

children.title: Autograd & Backpropagation
children.description: PyTorch builds a dynamic computation graph and differentiates it automatically. Misunderstanding requires_grad or detach causes gradient leaks or zero gradients that silently prevent learning.

children.title: nn.Module & Model Architecture
children.description: All PyTorch models subclass nn.Module; its parameter registration and forward pass contract enable saving, loading, and modifying models cleanly. Breaking this contract makes serialisation fail silently.

children.title: Loss Functions & Optimisers
children.description: Choosing the wrong loss (e.g. MSE for classification) or a poorly tuned learning rate are the most common reasons models fail to converge. Understanding the gradient update equation prevents wasted compute.

children.title: Dataset & DataLoader API
children.description: The Dataset / DataLoader contract decouples data loading from training and enables batching, shuffling, and multiprocessing. A poorly written Dataset becomes the GPU bottleneck, halving throughput.

children.title: torchvision Transforms
children.description: torchvision provides composable image transforms for resizing, normalising, and augmenting images. Normalising with incorrect mean/std shifts input distribution and degrades convergence significantly.

children.title: Training & Validation Loop
children.description: A correct training loop calls zero_grad, forward, loss.backward, and optimizer.step in order, and switches between train() and eval() modes. Skipping eval() keeps dropout and batch norm active during validation, producing inflated scores.

children.title: GPU Training & Mixed Precision
children.description: Moving tensors to CUDA and using torch.cuda.amp.autocast can deliver 2-3x speedups. Forgetting to move labels to the same device as the model is the most common error that crashes training.

cv-engineer.title: Convolutional Neural Networks
cv-engineer.description: CNNs are the backbone of image models. Understand convolutions and the architectures that won ImageNet.
cv-engineer.challenge: Fine-tune a pretrained ResNet on a custom image dataset and report accuracy.

children.title: Convolutional Layers
children.description: Convolutions share weights spatially to detect local patterns, making them far more parameter-efficient than fully connected layers on images. Misunderstanding padding and stride leads to unexpected output size errors that break architectures.

children.title: Pooling & Receptive Fields
children.description: Max pooling provides spatial down-sampling and translation invariance; the receptive field determines what spatial context each neuron sees. Stacking too many or too few layers gives models blind spots or wastes parameters.

children.title: ResNet & Skip Connections
children.description: Residual connections let gradients flow directly to shallow layers, solving the vanishing gradient problem that plagued deep networks. Without them, networks deeper than ~20 layers train worse than shallower ones.

children.title: Batch Normalisation
children.description: Batch norm standardises layer inputs, stabilises training, and acts as a regulariser. Freezing batch norm statistics incorrectly during fine-tuning causes train/inference discrepancy and degrades accuracy.

resources.label: Dive into Deep Learning — Batch Norm

children.title: Fine-Tuning Pretrained CNNs
children.description: ImageNet-pretrained weights give a powerful head start; fine-tuning the last few layers on your data typically outperforms training from scratch with far less data. Unfreezing too many layers on a small dataset causes catastrophic forgetting.

children.title: Feature Extraction with CNNs
children.description: Frozen CNN backbones can be used as fixed feature extractors for downstream tasks without any gradient flow. Choosing the right layer depth to extract from determines whether features are too local or too semantic.

children.title: Grad-CAM & Saliency Maps
children.description: Grad-CAM visualises which image regions activate a CNN's prediction, enabling model debugging and trust building. Without visualisation, overfitting to spurious correlations goes undetected until deployment.

children.title: Filter & Feature Map Visualisation
children.description: Visualising learned filters and intermediate feature maps helps diagnose dead neurons, poor generalisation, and whether the model is learning meaningful representations or surface statistics.

cv-engineer.title: Image Classification
cv-engineer.description: Master the canonical CV task. Build robust image classifiers with augmentation and solid evaluation.
cv-engineer.challenge: Build a classifier with augmentation that generalizes well, and analyze its mistakes.

children.title: End-to-End Classification Pipeline
children.description: A production classification pipeline covers data loading, transforms, model, loss, evaluation, and inference in a reproducible way. Ad-hoc scripts without clear train/val/test splits produce leaky evaluation and overestimated accuracy.

children.title: Data Augmentation
children.description: Random flips, crops, colour jitter, and mixup artificially expand training data and improve generalisation. Augmenting validation data or leaking test-time augmentation into metrics inflates reported performance.

children.title: Handling Class Imbalance
children.description: Weighted sampling, focal loss, and class-weighted cross-entropy stop models from ignoring minority classes. Ignoring imbalance causes the model to trivially predict the majority class and achieve misleadingly high accuracy.

children.title: Vision Transformers (ViT) Introduction
children.description: ViT treats an image as a sequence of patches and applies self-attention, matching or exceeding CNNs when trained on large datasets. Applying ViT to small datasets without pre-training typically underperforms CNNs significantly.

children.title: Classification Metrics
children.description: Accuracy, precision, recall, F1, and ROC-AUC each capture different failure modes. Reporting only accuracy on imbalanced data gives a false sense of model quality and hides the model ignoring rare classes.

children.title: Confusion Matrix Analysis
children.description: A confusion matrix shows which classes are most confused, guiding targeted data collection and model improvements. Without it, you cannot tell whether errors are random or systematic, making debugging guesswork.

children.title: Systematic Error Analysis
children.description: Inspecting misclassified examples reveals data quality issues, label errors, and distribution gaps that metrics alone cannot surface. Skipping error analysis leads to addressing the wrong problems in the next iteration.

children.title: Learning Curves & Overfitting Diagnosis
children.description: Plotting train vs validation loss over epochs reveals underfitting, overfitting, and optimal early stopping points. Without learning curves, you waste training time or deploy overfit models.

cv-engineer.title: Object Detection
cv-engineer.description: Find and localize objects. Learn detection concepts and build real detectors with YOLO.
cv-engineer.challenge: Train a YOLO model on a custom dataset to detect a class of objects and measure its mAP.

children.title: Bounding Boxes & IoU
children.description: Bounding boxes are axis-aligned rectangles in (x, y, w, h) or (x1, y1, x2, y2) format; Intersection over Union measures overlap quality. Getting the coordinate convention wrong shifts all boxes and breaks every downstream metric.

children.title: Anchors & Non-Max Suppression
children.description: Anchor boxes define the prior shapes a detector predicts offsets from; NMS removes duplicate overlapping predictions. Misconfigured anchors cause the model to predict the wrong aspect ratios and sizes for your object classes.

children.title: Training YOLO on Custom Data
children.description: Ultralytics YOLO provides a full pipeline from labelled datasets to trained weights in a few lines. Incorrect data.yaml class counts or label format mismatches silently corrupt training and produce random predictions.

children.title: YOLO Inference & Export
children.description: Ultralytics predict mode handles batching, confidence thresholds, and result parsing. Misunderstanding the confidence vs IOU threshold trade-off causes either too many false positives or missed detections in production.

children.title: mAP — Mean Average Precision
children.description: mAP@0.5 and mAP@0.5:0.95 are the standard detection benchmarks; they integrate precision over recall at different IoU thresholds. Evaluating with only a single threshold hides poor localisation quality.

children.title: Detection Debugging & Visualisation
children.description: Visualising predictions vs ground truth on validation samples reveals anchor mismatches, class confusion, and missed small objects. Relying solely on aggregate metrics hides systematic failure modes.

children.title: FPS Optimisation for Detection
children.description: Detection speed depends on input resolution, model size, and batch size; profiling exposes the actual bottleneck. Guessing at optimisations without measurement wastes engineering time on non-bottleneck stages.

children.title: Multi-Class & Multi-Label Detection
children.description: Multi-class detection assigns one label per box; multi-label detection allows overlapping classes. Conflating the two when defining your dataset causes the loss function to optimise the wrong objective.

cv-engineer.title: Segmentation & Advanced CV
cv-engineer.description: Go beyond boxes to pixel-level understanding. Learn semantic and instance segmentation.
cv-engineer.challenge: Build a semantic segmentation model that masks a target object class in images.

children.title: Semantic vs Instance Segmentation
children.description: Semantic segmentation assigns a class to every pixel; instance segmentation additionally distinguishes individual object instances. Choosing the wrong task formulation means collecting the wrong annotation format and using the wrong loss.

children.title: U-Net & Encoder-Decoder Architecture
children.description: U-Net's skip connections preserve spatial detail lost during downsampling, which is essential for fine boundary prediction. Without skip connections, segmentation masks are coarse and miss thin structures.

children.title: Mask R-CNN for Instance Segmentation
children.description: Mask R-CNN extends Faster R-CNN with a mask head that predicts per-instance pixel masks. Misaligned RoIAlign operations cause mask predictions to be spatially offset from the detected boxes.

children.title: Segment Anything Model (SAM)
children.description: SAM is a foundation model for interactive and zero-shot segmentation, prompted by points, boxes, or masks. Using SAM without understanding its prompt interface leads to ambiguous or multi-object segment outputs.

children.title: IoU & Dice Score
children.description: IoU (Jaccard) and Dice coefficient are the standard per-class segmentation metrics; mean IoU averages across classes. Optimising cross-entropy loss without monitoring IoU hides poor boundary performance on small classes.

children.title: Panoptic Segmentation Overview
children.description: Panoptic segmentation unifies semantic (stuff) and instance (things) segmentation into a single consistent output. Confusing stuff and things when building annotation pipelines creates incompatible label formats that break training.

children.title: Pose Estimation Overview
children.description: Pose estimation detects keypoints (joints, landmarks) on bodies or objects and infers their structural configuration. Confusing 2D heatmap-based and direct regression approaches leads to incorrect post-processing and wrong keypoint ordering.

children.title: Depth Estimation Basics
children.description: Monocular depth estimation predicts per-pixel depth from a single image using learned priors; stereo uses geometry. Not understanding the difference between relative and metric depth causes incorrect scale in downstream 3D applications.

cv-engineer.title: Datasets, Augmentation & Training
cv-engineer.description: Great models need great data and disciplined training. Build datasets and reproducible training runs.
cv-engineer.challenge: Build and version a labeled dataset, then run tracked training experiments comparing augmentations.

children.title: Annotation Tools & Formats
children.description: COCO JSON, YOLO TXT, and Pascal VOC XML are the dominant label formats; each tool exports differently. Exporting in the wrong format or with off-by-one coordinate conventions silently misaligns all your annotations.

children.title: Dataset Versioning with Roboflow
children.description: Roboflow tracks dataset versions including augmentation settings, class splits, and preprocessing, making experiments reproducible. Without versioning, different team members train on different dataset snapshots and results are incomparable.

children.title: Albumentations for CV Augmentation
children.description: Albumentations applies spatial and pixel-level transforms jointly to images and their masks, boxes, or keypoints. Applying spatial augmentations to images without transforming the corresponding labels corrupts the entire training set.

children.title: Mixup, CutMix & Mosaic Augmentation
children.description: Mixup blends two images linearly; CutMix pastes patches; Mosaic combines four images into one. These require soft or multi-label targets and using them with hard labels causes a mismatch that degrades performance.

children.title: Weights & Biases for CV Experiments
children.description: W&B logs metrics, hyperparameters, images, and model checkpoints, enabling experiment comparison across runs. Without experiment tracking, tuning hyperparameters becomes a guessing game with no reproducible baseline.

children.title: Reproducible Training
children.description: Seeding random number generators, pinning library versions, and logging exact hyperparameters makes experiments reproducible. Non-reproducible results make it impossible to confirm whether improvements are real or due to randomness.

children.title: Efficient Data Loading at Scale
children.description: Large CV datasets require WebDataset sharding, memory-mapped files, or streaming to avoid I/O bottlenecks. A DataLoader that cannot keep the GPU fed wastes 50-80% of available training compute.

children.title: Active Learning for CV
children.description: Active learning selects the most informative unlabelled samples to annotate, reducing labelling cost by 5-10x. Randomly sampling from a large unlabelled pool wastes annotation budget on redundant or easy examples.

cv-engineer.title: Deployment & Edge Inference
cv-engineer.description: Run vision models where they're needed. Optimize and deploy to servers and edge devices.
cv-engineer.challenge: Export your detector to ONNX and run real-time inference on a webcam stream.

children.title: Exporting to ONNX
children.description: ONNX is a framework-agnostic model format that enables deployment on any runtime without a PyTorch dependency. Dynamic axes must be declared correctly or fixed-batch models crash on variable-size production inputs.

children.title: Quantisation & Model Compression
children.description: INT8 quantisation reduces model size by 4x and inference latency by 2-4x with minimal accuracy loss if done correctly. Quantising without calibration data produces poorly scaled weights that devastate accuracy.

children.title: TensorRT Engine Building
children.description: TensorRT compiles ONNX models into hardware-optimised engines that fuse layers and apply mixed-precision, achieving 3-10x speedups over PyTorch on NVIDIA GPUs. Engine files are device-specific and cannot be transferred between GPU models.

children.title: ONNX Runtime Inference
children.description: ONNX Runtime provides optimised inference across CPU, CUDA, and DirectML execution providers without requiring the training framework. Choosing the wrong execution provider silently falls back to CPU, losing all GPU acceleration.

children.title: Deployment on NVIDIA Jetson
children.description: Jetson devices run TensorRT natively with CUDA support, making them the standard edge platform for real-time CV. Not accounting for Jetson's shared memory architecture causes OOM errors that do not occur on desktop GPUs.

children.title: Mobile Deployment with TFLite & CoreML
children.description: TFLite and CoreML are the native inference runtimes for Android and iOS; CoreML leverages the Neural Engine for further speedups. Exporting without delegate-compatible ops forces CPU fallback, making models too slow for real-time use.

children.title: Serving CV Models as APIs
children.description: FastAPI and Triton Inference Server expose models as REST or gRPC endpoints with batching and concurrency. Serialising raw NumPy arrays over HTTP rather than using efficient binary formats creates latency and bandwidth bottlenecks.

children.title: Production Monitoring & Drift Detection
children.description: Monitoring prediction confidence distributions and input statistics detects data drift before accuracy degrades visibly. Models silently degrade in production when lighting, camera, or scene conditions shift from training data.

llm-engineer.title: LLM Foundations
llm-engineer.description: Understand what large language models are and how they work at a practical level before building with them.
topics: How LLMs are trained & predict tokens
llm-engineer.challenge: Compare outputs of an open and a closed model on the same prompts and document the differences.

children.title: Transformer Architecture
children.description: LLMs are built on the transformer architecture: attention heads learn which tokens matter to each other, enabling the model to capture long-range dependencies. Without this mental model you will misread context-window limits, misinterpret failure modes, and misjudge what fine-tuning can fix.

children.title: Tokenization & Context Windows
children.description: Models never see raw text — they see token IDs. Token count determines cost, latency, and what fits in a single call. Misunderstanding tokenization leads to silently truncated inputs and hard-to-debug pricing surprises.

children.title: Temperature & Sampling
children.description: Temperature, top-p, and top-k control how deterministic or creative the model is. Getting this wrong causes either boring, repetitive output or unpredictable hallucinations — depending on which way you err.

children.title: Hallucination & Grounding
children.description: LLMs generate plausible-sounding text that can be factually wrong. Understanding why hallucinations occur — and how grounding through retrieval mitigates them — is the foundation of every production safeguard you will build.

children.title: Closed API Models
children.description: GPT-4, Claude, and Gemini are accessible via API with no infrastructure overhead. Knowing their capability tiers, rate limits, and pricing structures is essential for architectural decisions and cost estimation from day one.

children.title: Open-Weight Models
children.description: Llama, Mistral, and Qwen can be self-hosted, fine-tuned, and run offline. Understanding the trade-off between control, cost, and operational burden lets you choose the right model class for each use case.

children.title: Capabilities & Limitations
children.description: Knowing what LLMs can reliably do (text generation, summarization, classification) vs. where they fail (arithmetic, long-form memory, factual recall) saves you from building systems that rely on capabilities the model does not actually have.

children.title: Training & Prediction Loop
children.description: A model learns by predicting the next token, minimizing cross-entropy loss over billions of examples. Without this understanding you cannot reason about why a model succeeds or fails on edge cases, or what additional training data could help.

llm-engineer.title: Prompting & Context
llm-engineer.description: Prompting is the primary interface to LLMs. Learn to steer models reliably with context and examples.
llm-engineer.challenge: Design a prompt template with few-shot examples that produces consistent structured output.

children.title: System, User & Assistant Roles
children.description: The three-turn structure (system, user, assistant) is the primary control mechanism for every major chat model. Misusing it — for example placing instructions only in the user turn — leads to inconsistent model behavior across sessions.

children.title: Prompt Templates & Variables
children.description: Hardcoded prompts are a maintenance anti-pattern. Template systems with named variables let you version, test, and swap prompt logic independently from application code, which is critical for iterating in production.

children.title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting
children.description: Asking the model to reason step-by-step before giving an answer dramatically improves accuracy on complex tasks. Without CoT, you are leaving substantial reasoning capability on the table for any multi-step problem.

children.title: Structured Outputs
children.description: Forcing JSON or XML output with schema constraints makes LLM responses machine-readable and eliminates fragile regex parsing. Without structured outputs, downstream code breaks any time the model changes its phrasing.

children.title: Few-Shot Examples
children.description: Providing labeled input-output pairs directly in the prompt teaches the model the task format without any weight updates. Getting few-shot selection wrong — too many examples, low-quality ones, or wrong ordering — wastes tokens and often harms performance.

children.title: Context Window Management
children.description: Every token in the context window costs money and adds latency. Knowing how to trim, summarize, or paginate long conversations keeps your application within budget and prevents silent truncation that corrupts model reasoning.

children.title: Prompt Regression Testing
children.description: Prompts drift silently — a model update or slight wording change can break behavior that worked last week. Keeping a prompt test suite with expected outputs catches regressions before they reach users.

children.title: Prompt Injection Awareness
children.description: Malicious user input can override your system prompt and hijack the model's behavior. Understanding injection attack patterns is essential before any prompt-based system is exposed to untrusted users.

resources.label: OWASP Top 10 for LLMs — Prompt Injection

llm-engineer.title: Embeddings & Vector Databases
llm-engineer.description: Retrieval is what makes LLMs useful on private data. Learn embeddings and vector search.
llm-engineer.challenge: Build an embedding index over a document set with chunking and metadata filtering.

children.title: What Embeddings Are
children.description: Embeddings are dense numeric representations of text where semantic similarity maps to geometric closeness. Without understanding how embedding models are trained and what they encode, you cannot make informed choices about model selection or interpret retrieval failures.

children.title: Choosing an Embedding Model
children.description: Model dimension, max sequence length, and domain specificity vary widely across embedding models. Choosing the wrong model — for example one optimized for code when you need medical text — degrades retrieval accuracy in ways that are hard to debug.

children.title: ANN Search & Indexing
children.description: Approximate nearest-neighbor algorithms (HNSW, IVF) allow sub-millisecond similarity search over millions of vectors. Without understanding how indexes are built and their accuracy/speed tradeoffs, you cannot tune retrieval for production workloads.

children.title: Metadata Filtering & Hybrid Search
children.description: Pure vector search often returns semantically similar but contextually wrong results. Adding keyword filters and metadata constraints (date, author, category) narrows results to what is actually relevant, which is essential for enterprise retrieval pipelines.

children.title: Splitting Documents for Retrieval
children.description: How you split documents into chunks directly controls retrieval precision. Chunks too large waste context; chunks too small lose meaning. Recursive, semantic, and sentence-level splitting each suit different document types.

children.title: Overlap & Boundary Effects
children.description: Chunk boundaries can cut sentences mid-thought, breaking the coherence the embedding model was trained on. Overlap windows preserve context at boundaries, but too much overlap inflates index size and retrieval noise.

children.title: Indexing at Scale
children.description: Batch ingestion, incremental updates, and index maintenance become non-trivial above millions of documents. Planning for update pipelines and stale-index detection up front prevents costly full re-indexing later.

children.title: Similarity Metrics
children.description: Cosine similarity, dot product, and Euclidean distance give different results depending on whether embeddings are normalized. Picking the wrong metric for your embedding model can silently degrade ranking quality.

llm-engineer.title: RAG Architecture
llm-engineer.description: Build retrieval-augmented generation systems that answer from your data with citations.
llm-engineer.challenge: Build a production-style RAG system with re-ranking and source citations over a knowledge base.

children.title: RAG Architecture Overview
children.description: RAG separates knowledge from parameters: retrieve relevant documents at query time, then inject them into the prompt. Without this architectural understanding, teams often try to bake knowledge into fine-tuned weights — an expensive mistake that makes updates slow.

children.title: Context Assembly & Prompting
children.description: Retrieved chunks must be assembled into a coherent prompt that instructs the model to answer only from the provided context. Poorly assembled context causes the model to blend retrieved facts with parametric knowledge, producing unreliable citations.

children.title: Retrieval Strategies & Re-ranking
children.description: First-stage retrieval (vector search) is recall-oriented; re-ranking with a cross-encoder improves precision by scoring query-document pairs jointly. Skipping re-ranking means your RAG answers are only as good as your approximate nearest-neighbor index.

children.title: Evaluating Retrieval Quality
children.description: Precision, recall, and mean reciprocal rank tell you whether the right chunks are being retrieved before you even involve the LLM. Without retrieval-level metrics, you cannot tell whether a bad answer is caused by poor retrieval or poor generation.

children.title: Source Attribution
